title: Async functions return Promise<T>
instruction:
An async function always returns a Promise. Annotate the return type as `Promise<T>` to be explicit about what it resolves to.

Run this.
expectedOutput: Async functions always return a Promise
successMessage: The `await` keyword pauses execution until the Promise resolves. Outside an async function, you'd receive a `Promise<string>` instead of the value.

title: Error handling with unknown
instruction:
In TypeScript, caught errors are typed as `unknown`. You must narrow the type before accessing properties.

Run this.
successMessage: Always check `error instanceof Error` before accessing `.message`. TypeScript types caught errors as `unknown` to force this check.

title: Promise.all for parallel execution
instruction:
`Promise.all` runs multiple promises in parallel and waits for all to complete. The result type is inferred from the input types.

Run this.
successMessage: `Promise.all` resolves when ALL promises resolve. TypeScript infers the tuple type from the array — `[number, number, number]` here.
